regions.text: John Amos Comenius (1592-1670) was the first to use pictures for teaching languages. He almost became the first head of Harvard.
His famous book, 'Orbis Pictus' (1657), started with this image. (Illustration: A teacher and a young student).
(Illustration: A 17th-century teacher instructing a boy in an open field, with a town in the distance).
The teacher: 'Come, Boy, learn to be wise.' The boy: 'What is it to be wise?' The teacher: 'To understand, act, and speak rightly.'
